summary_one_line: A well-formed XML document whose body contains an entity reference '&name;' to an undeclared entity, keeping ctxt->wellFormed==1 and ent==NULL at the blocker

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Byte-exact XML structural tokens (the '<?xml version="1.0"?>' prolog and '<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/...' markup) must appear unbroken at the document head so the parser keeps wellFormed==1 long enough to parse an entity reference whose name resolves to no declared entity (ent==NULL).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The cmplog winning seed begins with a clean '<?xml version="1' / '.0"?>' / '<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/1' run, exactly the literal tokens libxml2 compares against; this preserves well-formed parsing into xmlParseEntityRef where the true side is taken (W L7658 T=3).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser seeds carry corrupting prefixes (binary '=\x06\x00\x00\x00', '____wu', 'EME=') ahead of and inside the prolog, so the parser errors out early and the entity-ref path is reached far less often (L L7658 F=86, T=0 on the blocker side).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads intercepted CMP operands (the literal prolog/markup keyword comparisons) from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the expected literal at the matching input offsets, repairing the structural tokens that naive havoc can only stumble onto by luck.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same structural requirement: the prolog/markup keyword comparisons must be driven to (or near) a match so parsing stays well-formed up to the undeclared entity reference.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile keeps any input that brings a CMP-operand pair closer to matching than previously seen, so partial matches on the '<?xml'/'version='/'DOCTYPE' comparisons are retained as corpus and incrementally completed, letting it resolve the branch in 9/10 trials.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive only retains inputs on new edge buckets, so a half-correct keyword that hits no new edge is discarded; the loser stays blocked in 8/10 trials with the corrupted-prefix seeds never completing the structural tokens.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient: each integer/string CMP writes a Hamming/prefix-distance bucket, and a closer-matching input adds a new bucket and is preserved, giving a coverage gradient toward the exact prolog/markup literals that naive lacks.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same structural requirement; no comparison-solving aid, so the win comes from spending more mutation energy on the small well-formed XML seeds that already carry most of the prolog/markup.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: minimizer's calibration-derived power schedule gives faster, smaller, more-stable seeds more havoc mutations per visit, so the compact valid-XML seeds (e.g. the 257-258 byte ones) get heavily mutated and complete the entity-ref path, resolving 8/10.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive runs the same havoc/token stack under a plain mutational stage with no energy weighting, spreading mutations uniformly and leaving the structural tokens unrepaired; it stays blocked 8/10.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: minimizer's calibrated energy (StdPowerMutationalStage with calibration-based perf_score, PowerSchedule None) concentrates havoc effort on small/stable well-formed seeds, raising the chance of completing the valid-XML structure without any CMP introspection.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the well-formed-and-undeclared-entity test inside xmlParseEntityRef; the globally blocked side is the true branch.
Globally blocked side is the true branch.
The cmplog winner seed contains a clean XML prolog and DOCTYPE markup run at the document head.
The winner seed continues with valid version and DOCTYPE SYSTEM markup.
Loser seeds carry corrupting binary/garbage prefixes ahead of the prolog.
Another loser seed begins with non-XML ascii junk before the prolog.
On the blocker line the winner takes the true side while the loser takes only the false side many more times.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L7658  T=3 F=0  T=0 F=86  if ((ctxt->wellFormed == 1 ) && (ent == NULL) &&  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog wins the pair against naive with a large probability divergence under the I2S delta.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which scans the input for an operand match and substitutes the other operand.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile wins the pair against naive under the value_profile delta.
value_profile retains an input that produces a closer CMP-operand pair as a new CMP_MAP bucket.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: minimizer wins the pair against naive under the calibrated_energy delta.
minimizer derives per-seed mutation count from a calibration-based perf_score favoring faster, smaller, more-stable seeds.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
faster, smaller,
more-stable seeds earn more mutations

evidence_trail.claim: naive only retains inputs that hit a new edge bucket, with no CMP introspection.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a new bucket triggers a
corpus-add

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 6690 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 339ba30b2f9d5965` and observed the I2S-floor signal: 2 ancestor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 3 (54f80d8c2818efaa) and depth 4 (8c7bd7ba6dfb824c), characteristic of unwrapped I2SRandReplace finds in cmplog under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on the same well-formed-prolog-then-undeclared-entity structure where cmplog does NOT outpace naive in reaching the gated branch (no I2S advantage), refuting the claim that the literal XML structural tokens are the comparison-solvable feature driving the divergence.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact comparison(s) that gate well-formedness are inferred from the prolog/DOCTYPE bytes in the seeds and source, not directly observed as named CMP operands in the prompt, so the precise I2S-solved literal is uncertain.
